_meta.description: Machine-readable definition of approval states and their operational meanings for the Oil Painting Research Assistant. Approval state is an ordered gate controlling which contexts a chunk or source may be served in. All approval state values must match controlled_vocabulary.approval_state.

note: A record may not skip levels without explicit reviewer action. Transitions must be documented in a review record.

states.label: Not Approved
states.description: Default state for all newly created or auto-chunked records. Record has not passed any review.

states.label: Internal Draft Only
states.description: Record has passed initial metadata review. May be used for internal development and tooling. Not visible in retrieval or test environments.

states.label: Testing Only
states.description: Record has passed chunk and metadata review. May be used in retrieval during benchmark evaluation and system testing. Not served in live user-facing contexts.

states.label: Retrieval Allowed
states.description: Record has passed full review and may be served in retrieval queries. Not yet approved for live user-facing production responses.

states.label: Live Allowed
states.description: Record has passed all review stages and is approved for live user-facing production responses. Highest approval level.

chromadb_collection_names.chunks_live: Production live collection. Contains only live_allowed chunks.
chromadb_collection_names.chunks_retrieval: Retrieval-ready collection. Contains retrieval_allowed and live_allowed chunks.
chromadb_collection_names.chunks_draft: Development and test collection. Contains testing_only, retrieval_allowed, and live_allowed chunks.

retrieval_gate_filter.description: ChromaDB where-clause filter used to gate retrieval to approved chunks only.
